You are an opponent-modeling assistant for the CaSiNo negotiation game.
Two players each pick up 3 packages of Food, Water, and Firewood for an
upcoming camping trip. Each player ranks the three items as High, Medium,
or Low priority (a permutation of the three).

You will see one player's perspective: their own priority ranking, their
free-text reasons for those priorities, and the dialogue so far (with
``Me:`` for that player and ``Opponent:`` for the other).

From this you must infer two things:

1. ``prefs``: the OPPONENT's priority ordering as
   ``[top, mid, low]`` over {Food, Water, Firewood}.
2. ``satisfaction``: how satisfied the player you are speaking for will
   be at the end of the negotiation. Pick exactly one of:
   "Extremely dissatisfied", "Slightly dissatisfied", "Undecided",
   "Slightly satisfied", "Extremely satisfied".

Reply with JSON only, no prose, in exactly this shape:

{"prefs": ["<item>", "<item>", "<item>"], "satisfaction": "<label>"}

Items must be a permutation of Food, Water, Firewood (no repeats, no
extras). Satisfaction must be one of the five exact strings above.

Your own priority ranking:
  High:   Firewood
  Medium: Food
  Low:    Water

Your own reasons for those priorities:
  High: I am afraid of the dark and don't want my fire to die off.
  Medium: I would like to be able to have a snack at a later time and this would allow me to do that.
  Low: I don't drink much water so I don't really need much of that.

Dialogue so far:
Opponent: Hi, are you excited for the camping trip?

  High:   Firewood
  Medium: Food
  Low:    Water

Dialogue so far:
Opponent: Hello I would like to take 2 firewood packages 2 food packages and 2 water packages. I am camping in the desert with 30 + kids on a school trip and we had a few more children come on the trip last min and need to make sure they are warm fed and hydrated.
Me: I'm sorry, I couldn't possibly give you the majority of everything. I have a large group myself, including a baby that I need to keep warm. I need to have 2 of the firewood, and I can give you two of either the food or the water, but not two of both.
Opponent: how old is your baby ?
Me: she's 6 months old. so i really need to keep her warm.
Opponent: ok well given that the 6 month old babies main nutrients comes from breastmilk or formula and you have to hold the baby to feed them plus you should have blanket for the baby which will keep the baby warm. I will consider taking 1 firewood and 2 foods and 1 water. 
Me: I think that should be OK. So I get 2 firewood, one food, and 2 water.
Opponent: Yes i can agree to that! 🙂 I sure do miss having a 6 month old baby to snuggle lol. mine is 2 and is not interested in snuggling anymore.

Me: A few times.  I'd be willing to share my fire with you if you don't know how to light one of your own.
Opponent: I was hoping you would have extra firewood to spare with me. I could trade some for some water.
Me: Here's the problem.  I'll build and tend the fire but I'll need two packages to do that.  But I'll only need one water package.  
Opponent: I don't really need that much water either, but I need extra firewood because I'm anemic and alway feel cold. Do you have extra food I could trade as well?
Me: Oh, wow.  You're anemic.  I'm diabetic so I need to eat frequently so that I don't have a low blood sugar episode.  I can exchange give you one food package, two firewoods and one water.
Opponent: That sounds perfect 🙂 I'm also hoping to make some smores with the grandkids one night. I'm so excited.
Me: So I keep 2 food, 1 firewood and 2 waters. Is that right?
Opponent: Yes, that is correct. Thank you so much!
